meta.name: Half Intermediate
meta.subtitle: Break 1:45 — 5:00/km for 21.1km
meta.description: For runners with a solid base who want to break 1:45 for the half marathon. Twenty weeks of threshold-focused training, weekly track sessions and long runs building to 22km. Pfitzinger-influenced in structure.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-1:45 requires 5:00/km for 21km. The threshold runs are the engine of this plan — they directly build your ability to sustain 5:00/km. The long runs build the endurance to sustain it to the line. Do not sacrifice the long run for extra midweek volume.

weeks.title: Base assessment
weeks.note: First week. Tuesday assesses your interval fitness; Thursday establishes your threshold feel.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 5x1km @ current HM pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 11km

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.
500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.
Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.note: Second week. The 800m reps develop race-pace sharpness.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-1:45 attempt
weeks.note: Race week.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.
500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.
500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 4x100m strides · Race: through 5km at 5:02, settle to 5:00, build from 15km · Gel at 7km and 14km · Negative split target

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or two slices of toast with peanut butter. 600ml water. Stick entirely to foods you have eaten before long runs — half marathon nutrition starts with breakfast.
300ml water or isotonic drink. Do not gulp — sip steadily. If you normally use caffeine, this is the time.
10–15min easy jog. 4x100m strides. The warm-up is essential for a half marathon — you will not be running yourself into it in the first mile.
Gel with water at the mile 6 or 7 station. Take it with a full cup of water, not a sip. If you have not trained with gels, take water only.
Second gel at mile 10 if you trained with two gels. Otherwise water only. Do not take a gel you have not practised with.
Water or isotonic drink at the finish line. 500ml in the first 10 minutes. Keep moving — walk for at least 10 minutes to flush lactic acid.
Banana and protein bar or shake. Start the recovery process immediately — you have just run 21km.
Within 90 minutes: protein (30–40g), carbohydrates, vegetables. 1.5g protein per kg bodyweight today. Drink consistently through the afternoon — 500ml extra per hour for the first 3 hours.
